contents.value: **if** — CoffeeScript keyword.
**else** — CoffeeScript keyword.
**unless** — CoffeeScript keyword.
**then** — CoffeeScript keyword.
**for** — CoffeeScript keyword.
**console.log** — Built-in function/command in CoffeeScript.
**console.error** — Built-in function/command in CoffeeScript.
**console.warn** — Built-in function/command in CoffeeScript.
**setTimeout** — Built-in function/command in CoffeeScript.
**setInterval** — Built-in function/command in CoffeeScript.
A **function** is a reusable block of code that performs a specific task.
A **variable** stores a value that can be referenced and modified.
A **loop** repeats a block of code while a condition is true or for each item in a collection.
A **conditional** executes different code branches based on a boolean test.
A **string** is a sequence of characters used for text.
An **array** (or list) is an ordered collection of elements.
A **dictionary** (hash/map) stores key-value pairs for fast lookup.
A **class** defines a blueprint for creating objects with attributes and methods.
A **module** is a file containing reusable code that can be imported.
An **exception** signals an error condition that can be caught and handled.
A **lambda** is a concise anonymous function expression.
A **closure** captures variables from its enclosing scope.
An **iterator** yields values one at a time from a sequence.
A **generator** lazily produces values using yield.
A **decorator** wraps a function to extend its behavior.

```coffee
class Array
```
The built-in Array type in CoffeeScript (inherited from JavaScript) represents an ordered, integer-indexed collection of values. Arrays are dynamically sized and can hold mixed types. CoffeeScript adds syntactic sugar like list comprehensions, splats, and range-based slicing.

```coffee
class Object
```
The base prototype for all CoffeeScript/JavaScript objects, providing key-value storage and prototype-based inheritance. Objects are used as hash maps, configuration containers, and the foundation for class instances. CoffeeScript supports object literals with implicit braces and significant indentation.

```coffee
class String
```
The built-in String type representing a sequence of UTF-16 characters. CoffeeScript supports string interpolation with double-quoted strings using #{expression} syntax, heredoc strings with triple quotes for multi-line text, and block strings that preserve indentation.

```coffee
console: Object
```
The console object provides access to the browser or Node.js debugging console for outputting messages, warnings, errors, and performance timing information. It is the primary tool for runtime debugging and logging in CoffeeScript applications.

```coffee
Math: Object
```
The Math object provides standard mathematical constants (PI, E, LN2, etc.) and static functions for common mathematical operations including trigonometry, rounding, random number generation, exponentiation, and finding minimum/maximum values.

```coffee
require(module: String) -> module.exports
```
Imports a module in CoffeeScript (Node.js CommonJS modules). Takes a module identifier string which can be a core module name, a relative file path, or an npm package name. Returns the exported object or value from the module, enabling code reuse and dependency management.

```coffee
class Promise
```
Represents the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation.

```coffee
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```coffee
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```coffee
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```coffee
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```coffee
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```coffee
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
